data.body:
## Description

**What**: Run feasibility reviewer against v2-v3 migration epic specs retroactively. Run completion verifier against w21-24kl + 3-4 other past epics. Measure false-positive rate.
**Why**: Validates gate design before applying to new epics; calibrates false-positive rate to ensure the gates are useful, not noisy.
**Scope**:
- IN: Retroactive feasibility review (w21-24kl, w21-bwfw), retroactive completion verification (w21-24kl + 3-4 clean epics), false-positive measurement
- OUT: Ongoing operational metrics (tracked post-epic, not part of this story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, feasibility reviewer retroactively flags ACLI capability assumptions in w21-24kl/w21-bwfw ← Satisfies Validation
- When this story is complete, completion verifier retroactively catches incomplete v2 cleanup in w21-24kl ← Satisfies Validation
- When this story is complete, false-positive calibration across 4-5 past epics confirms correct pass/fail distinction ← Satisfies Validation
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] w21-24kl and w21-bwfw ticket data must be accessible in current ticket system. If migration altered content format, fixture data must be reconstructed from git history.
- [Testing] Retroactive validation needs deterministic fixtures — use fixed input, evaluate output structure not content

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
